steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: “They are hardly pristine” is an independent clause. Here, “though” works as a sentence adverb meaning “however,” so it needs to be set off from the surrounding clauses.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
The keyed punctuation is “pristine, though;”: the comma after “pristine” sets off “though,” and the semicolon then breaks before the next independent clause.

“pristine; though” incorrectly places the semicolon before “though,” “pristine, though,” adds an unnecessary comma after “though,” and “pristine, though” omits the semicolon needed before the next clause.
